preferred.indoor golf simulator: インドアゴルフシミュレーター

tone: friendly-professional, second-person, conversational. No exclamation marks. No emoji. Avoid marketing-speak inflation.
formality: 丁寧語 (teineigo) — です/ます調. Neither casual である調 nor honorific 尊敬語/謙譲語. Suitable for marketing copy aimed at adult customers. Use カタカナ for golf-domain loanwords (ゴルフ, シミュレーター, レッスン, パッケージ); use 漢字 + ひらがな for general-purpose verbs and connectives.
preserve: All numerals as half-width Arabic digits (do not convert to ２３４)

terminology.note: Long-vowel ー form throughout the corpus. Caddie fee = キャディーフィー; caddie tip = キャディーへのチップ.
green fee — single katakana compound, no interior space or long-vowel drop.

avoid: ドライビング レンジ

currency.format: Half-width Arabic digits immediately followed by THB, no space: 1,800THB, 550THB. Comma thousands separator.
currency.physical_cash_exception: Use バーツ (katakana) ONLY when referring to physical baht CASH as a currency object (e.g. タイバーツの現金 = 'Thai baht cash'), never for a price figure.
currency.note: RULING: guide/explainer content uses the THB abbreviation (269 THB occurrences vs 9 バーツ across JA guide prose). Legacy entries gg-baggage-fees-ja, exp-25-ja (bring-clubs), and exp-39-ja (rent-at-course) use バーツ for price figures — do NOT follow them for new content. messages/ja.json UI copy mixes THB and バーツ per its own namespace history; leave it as-is.

conventions.ranges: Wave dash 〜 (U+301C) between values, no spaces: 25〜30kg, 300〜500THB, 4〜7kg.
conventions.numerals: Half-width Arabic digits only; never full-width ２３４ or kanji numerals for figures.
conventions.as_of_format: （2026年7月現在） — parenthesized. 現在 is the majority form (10 uses vs 1 for 時点); use 現在.

honesty_constraints.required_scoping: Negative claims about coach languages must be scoped to LENGOLF (e.g. 'LENGOLFには日本人コーチや日本語でのレッスンはありませんが…'), never stated city-wide about Bangkok. City-wide statements may only be POSITIVE and general (e.g. 'バンコクでは英語での指導が一般的です').
honesty_constraints.allowed_language_support: 予約・事前のご相談はLINE @lengolf にて日本語で承ります (booking/consultation in Japanese via LINE @lengolf). Also: on-screen simulator numbers (ヘッドスピード, 打ち出し角) help across the language barrier. Do NOT claim Japanese-language lessons or Japanese coaches.
honesty_constraints.linter_hint: Flag each forbidden_claims string for human REVIEW (not auto-reject) when it appears WITHOUT LENGOLF-scoped negation nearby (はありません／ではありません within the same sentence or paragraph) AND the LINE @lengolf allowance. These are honesty rules, not lexical bans — the strings are legitimate when correctly scoped.

transliterations.The Mercury Ville: ザ・マーキュリービル
transliterations.note: In JA prose, BTS station and building names are transliterated to katakana (BTSチットロム駅, ザ・マーキュリービル4階). 'The Mercury Ville' / 'Mercury Ville' in Latin is also attested and acceptable in address-like contexts. Latin brand/product names are glossed once with katakana in parens on first use: Majesty（マジェスティ）. Keep LENGOLF, len.golf, BTS, MRT, PGA, LINE @lengolf, booking.len.golf, and Callaway verbatim. NOTE: transliterating Chidlom/Mercury Ville is intentional JA-prose style and diverges from the Latin-verbatim brand_immutable list (which governs the pure-TS lexicon check for UI strings) — do not 'correct' チットロム／ザ・マーキュリービル in shipped JA guide copy.

seo_norms.title_display_target: ~32 full-width characters before SERP truncation; front-load the target query, then a descriptive tail after an em dash — (shipped guide titles run 34–65 chars, acceptable for the tail).
seo_norms.meta_note: Put the hook and target query in the first ~80 characters; JP meta descriptions truncate around 120 full-width chars on mobile. Shipped guide metas run ~90–160 chars.
seo_norms.front_load: Lead with the reader's query (e.g. ゴルフクラブの受託手荷物料金), not the brand.

style_notes: Anti-repetition: vary sentence-final forms; do not end three consecutive sentences with です。 Mix in ます／体言止め where natural.
Half-width digits and Latin units (kg, THB) inside Japanese text; no space between number and unit (6kg, 1,800THB).
No exclamation marks, no emoji. Friendly-professional です/ます調 throughout.
Gloss a Latin brand/product name once with katakana in parens on first use (Majesty（マジェスティ）), then either form.
Em dash — (spaced) for asides; wave dash 〜 for numeric ranges.
